title: Applied Causal Inference
summary: Reference-quality causal-inference implementations in Julia and Python — DoubleML, time-series causal models, and instrumental variables, with explicit validation against known answers.
description_long: Dual-language causal methods codebase with tests and validation architecture. Implements DoubleML, instrumental variables, and time-series-aware variants in both Julia and Python. The intent is reference-quality implementations with explicit validation against synthetic data and known answers.
whats_next: Development paused at reference quality. Future direction: add additional estimators and integrate with the temporal-validation utilities.

title: double_ml_time_series
summary: A LaTeX manuscript + companion code for temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting, HAC inference, and synthetic examples. Web edition live.
description_long: Implementation and methodology notes for double machine learning extended to time-series settings. Covers temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting protocols that respect time ordering, and HAC-style inference. Synthetic examples illustrate where naive cross-fitting fails and what to do instead.
whats_next: All 10 chapters of the web edition are live (potential outcomes + Frisch–Waugh–Lovell through the research pipeline); the manuscript also ships as a PDF in the repo. Next: revision passes as the methodology evolves.

title: TemporalValidation / temporalcv
summary: Temporal validation utilities and methodology lineage. Walk-forward splits, purging, embargo windows, and leakage prevention.
description_long: Utilities for temporally-aware cross-validation: walk-forward splits with explicit gaps, purging adjacent observations, embargo windows, and other leakage-prevention patterns. The methodology lineage notes connect classical temporal CV practice to modern uses in causal and ML pipelines.
whats_next: Published to PyPI as temporalcv v2.3.0. Future: a Julia companion module with a consistent API.

title: Prompt-injection PoC
summary: Methodology proof-of-concept for detecting prompt-injection attacks against LLMs. Rigorous evaluation with bootstrap CIs on lift over baselines.
description_long: DeBERTa-v3 with LoRA, evaluated against a baseline ladder (heuristic → logistic regression → frozen encoder → fine-tuned) on locked holdout splits with bootstrap confidence intervals on lift. Calibration via temperature scaling; reliability and ECE reported on a model card. Framed explicitly as methodology proof-of-concept, not a production detector.
whats_next: Source repo is private — see the public OOD study and eval-toolkit below. V2 methodology complete; decision log finalized. Next: archive the PoC and migrate the methodology patterns into the eval-toolkit harness.

title: eval-toolkit
summary: Reusable binary-classification evaluation harness: baseline ladder, bootstrap CIs, calibration, stop-gates against overengineering.
description_long: Evaluation library for binary-classification AI/ML systems. Encodes the methodology used in prompt-injection-detector as reusable components: baseline ladders, bootstrap confidence intervals on metrics and lift, calibration tooling, and explicit stop-gates that fire before adding model complexity.
whats_next: Published to PyPI as eval-toolkit. Continuing: API hardening and calibration tooling, still behind explicit stop-gates.

title: prompt-injection portfolio
summary: Broader pre-alpha field log: ongoing prompt-injection research, attack catalogs, eval scenarios beyond the PoC's scope.
description_long: A pre-alpha companion to the prompt-injection PoC. Wider in scope: tracks attack categories, eval scenarios, OOD probes, and methodology variations that don't fit inside the PoC's locked evaluation protocol. Field-log format — assumes the reader has context.
whats_next: Pre-alpha. Direction: select the most-mature scenarios and migrate them into the eval-toolkit + PoC pipeline.

title: DeepLearning.AI Study Notes
summary: An online book corpus synthesized from deeplearning.ai short courses (in development — repo public, web deploy pending). Per-course .tex → MDX conversion with an audit-during-conversion quality pass. Searchable, math-rendered, with Anki flashcards.
description_long: In-progress corpus of study notes synthesized from DeepLearning.AI short courses. Each course is converted from LaTeX source through a quality-audit MDX conversion into a searchable, math-rendered, citation-tracked online book. Includes BibTeX bibliography integration, Pagefind search, KaTeX math, and Anki .apkg deck export. Built on book-scaffold-astro. Currently local-only — publishing surface pending.
whats_next: PoC for a broader pattern: a set of Gold-tier DeepLearning.AI courses targeted; further course-derived books from other sources (e.g., Bertsekas RL + control, ssm-foundations chapters) lined up. Deploy to a public URL is the immediate next step.

title: rl_and_control — RL + Control Theory guide
summary: Reinforcement learning + control theory from fundamentals to convergence — a structured syllabus with Python/JAX/Julia experiments, an indexed bibliography, and method-family dossiers. Companion to ssm-foundations.
description_long: In-progress authored guide bridging classical optimal control (Bellman, DP, MPC, LQR/LQG) with modern reinforcement learning (policy gradient, actor-critic, model-based, MPC-RL hybrids). A multi-part syllabus: RL Foundations -> Control Theory -> Convergence. Includes Python (PyTorch/Gymnasium/SB3), JAX (Brax, differentiable MPC), and Julia (ControlSystems.jl, JuMP MPC) experiment tracks. Bibliography: an indexed, status-tracked corpus across thematic sections, with method-family dossiers.
whats_next: Local-only; publishing surface (Astro/MDX web guide) planned, matching the ssm-foundations book-scaffold-astro pattern. Bibliography (rl_and_control/references/paper_index.md) is the data source for the /lab/research-graph/ demo (shipped 2026-05-24; last densified 2026-06-10 with pre-arXiv classics + control-text anchors).

external_context.label: Live demo: RL + Control citation graph

title: ssm-foundations
summary: A lens-led foundations book bridging numerical analysis and dynamical systems to modern sequence-model architectures — state-space models (SSMs), Mamba, S4, Hyena, DeltaNet. Live and in progress: the foundations chapters are authored; the structured-SSM and later chapters are being written.
description_long: Public foundations book derived from the post-transformers research repo. Chapters organized lens-first: continuous-time dynamics → discretization → stability theory → structured state-space models → selective scans (Mamba, S4, S4D) → hybrids. Each chapter pairs prose with JAX and Julia companions, reproducible figures, and Julia stability tests. Built on book-scaffold-astro v4 academic preset; deployed via Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets.
whats_next: Live at https://ssm-foundations.brandon-behring.dev. Foundations chapters authored; actively writing the structured-SSM layer (HiPPO, S4/S4D, Mamba) and the later 'beyond SSMs' chapters (linear attention, DeltaNet, xLSTM/RWKV).

title: book-scaffold-astro
summary: Astro template for long-form technical books, published to npm as @brandon_m_behring/book-scaffold-astro — MDX, Paged.js print, Tufte typography, Pagefind search, KaTeX. The scaffold under every book and guide here.
description_long: Reusable scaffold for searchable, math-rendered long-form books: Astro + MDX content, Paged.js print output, Tufte-style typography, and a Koller–Friedman chapter structure. Published to npm (as @brandon_m_behring/book-scaffold-astro) and consumed as a versioned dependency, so consumer books (double_ml_time_series, ssm-foundations, the guides) inherit upgrades by bumping a version. Maintained through a dogfooding loop — each consumer surfaces scaffold gaps that feed back as fixes.
whats_next: A reusable Provenance + audit component (Phase 6a) that surfaces the process-as-artifact trail on every consumer chapter.

title: deploy-workflows
summary: Reusable GitHub Actions workflow for deploying Astro sites to Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets. Every book and guide here pins a tagged version of it.
description_long: A single reusable deploy workflow that consumer repos call with secrets inherited: it runs the build and a Wrangler deploy to Cloudflare Workers Static Assets. Centralizing it means a deploy fix ships once and propagates to every consumer that pins the tag, instead of drifting across per-repo YAML. This site deploys through it.
whats_next: Tagged releases carry optional apt-package and PR-preview inputs; an OIDC-auth migration is queued for a later phase.

title: runpod-deploy
summary: Config-driven RunPod orchestration for reusable GPU project deployments — pod templates and run configs for compute-heavy experiments.
description_long: Infrastructure-as-config for GPU work: parameterized RunPod pod definitions and run configs so training and inference experiments launch reproducibly rather than through ad-hoc console clicks. Built to make the compute side of the research loop repeatable.
whats_next: Active; tightening the config schema and templates as more GPU experiments consume it.

title: guides
summary: Hub for a family of interview-prep guides for data science, ML, and AI engineering — live at guides.brandon-behring.dev, with per-guide companions mounted as subroutes.
description_long: Hub for a family of interview-prep guides aimed at data-science, ML-engineering, and AI-engineering readers: a landing page, methodology, and about section, with per-guide companion repos plugged in as subroutes. The architecture keeps each guide independently releasable while sharing one branded shell. The hub is live; AI engineering is the first shipped sub-guide, mounted at /ai-engineering/.
whats_next: AI engineering shipped as the first sub-guide; experimentation is the pilot; further guides on data fundamentals and ML systems follow under the hub.

title: guides-experimentation (pilot)
summary: Pilot guide under the guides hub: experimentation methodology — A/B testing, sample size and power, variance reduction, sequential testing.
description_long: The first companion guide for the hub: a practitioner treatment of experimentation — design, sample-size and power, variance reduction, sequential testing, and decision-making under cost asymmetry. Currently schema plus skeleton; the full chapter build lands in Phase 1.
whats_next: Full multi-chapter MDX build in Phase 1, then deploys under the hub's /experimentation/ subroute.

title: AI Engineering guide
summary: Practitioner guide to AI engineering — evaluation, LLM application engineering, production systems, and working effectively with AI. Four modules, 13 chapters each, live under the guides hub.
description_long: A four-module guide to building and shipping AI systems: evaluation and benchmarking (decision discipline, calibration, judge-based eval), LLM application engineering (prompting, retrieval, RAG, agents, system design), production AI systems (deployment, latency, cost, observability, drift, guardrails), and working with AI (verification, delegation, and provenance under automation). Built on book-scaffold-astro and shipped as the first sub-guide of the guides hub, with companion mini-libraries.
whats_next: Live at guides.brandon-behring.dev/ai-engineering/. Companion mini-libraries (mini_eval, mini_rag, mini_agent, mini_prod) ship alongside; further guides (data fundamentals, ML systems) follow under the hub.

title: mathematical-guides (family)
summary: A family of formal, Definition–Theorem–Proof guides for a mathematician audience — a hub plus per-subject siblings (transformers, reinforcement learning). Private while in development.
description_long: Formal guides written in a Definition–Theorem–Proof register for readers who want the mathematics first. A hub coordinates per-subject sibling guides — transformers and reinforcement learning so far — each a standalone Astro/MDX book deploying under the hub. The repositories are private while the structure and first chapters are built, so there's no public link yet.
whats_next: Deploy-configured for a mathematical.brandon-behring.dev subdomain (target still being confirmed); goes public as chapters reach a shippable state.

title: claude-books (family)
summary: A multi-book home for Claude Code / agentic-coding writing on book-scaffold-astro — live at claude-books.brandon-behring.dev: the Architect's Reference (cert study guide), Agentic Systems Design, the cross-tool Agentic Coding book, and a shared glossary, with a Claude Code handbook in progress.
description_long: A Claude Code / agentic-coding book ecosystem — a monorepo on book-scaffold-astro, deployed as one apex site with per-book subroutes. Live: the Architect's Reference (a self-contained CCA-F study guide, /architect/), Agentic Systems Design (engineering agentic systems, multi-volume, /design/), and the cross-tool 'Agentic Coding: Principles and Practices' book (/agentic-coding/, the 'using coding agents' companion); a Claude-Code-specific Handbook is in progress (served noindex until its v1.0). It supersedes earlier LaTeX drafts (claude-best-practices, claude-code-field-guide).
whats_next: Live at claude-books.brandon-behring.dev. Next: finish the Handbook (the 'Use' book) toward v1.0; the cert and design tracks keep deepening.

external_context.label: Agentic Coding — cross-tool (Claude Code · Gemini · Codex)

title: annuity-pricing
summary: Python library for annuity pricing and valuation — bond math, option Greeks, and Monte Carlo for fixed and variable annuity products.
description_long: A Python library for pricing and valuing annuity products: the bond-math foundation, Black–Scholes option Greeks, and Monte Carlo simulation for embedded guarantees. A companion Julia stack (AnnuityCore.jl, AnnuityProducts.jl, AnnuityData.jl) reimplements the core engine for performance and multiple dispatch. This reflects earlier domain work and is deliberately not the site's lead.
whats_next: Reference implementation; the Python package is configured (pyproject, tests, docs) and being readied for PyPI publication, with the companion Julia stack to follow.

title: insurance-ai-toolkit
summary: AI/ML toolkit for insurance work — VM-21-style reserve calculation and lapse-rate modeling.
description_long: A toolkit applying machine learning to actuarial problems: VM-21-style variable-annuity reserve calculation, stochastic lapse and mortality modeling, and policy-level risk assessment. It pairs with annuity-pricing for end-to-end valuation workflows.
whats_next: Active development on the lapse models and reserve backtesting.

title: research-kb
summary: Hybrid-search research knowledge base — BM25 + vector + citation graph over a large multi-domain corpus, exposed via MCP. The system powering the citation-graph demo here.
description_long: A hybrid-search knowledge base I built to accelerate my own research: BM25 full-text, vector embeddings, and a citation and concept graph over a large multi-domain corpus spanning causal inference, RL and control, and sequence models. It's exposed as MCP tools so an assistant can search and traverse it directly, and it's the data source behind the /lab/research-graph demo on this site. Infrastructure for my own learning, not a product.
whats_next: Densifying the citation graph and hardening corpus metadata quality across domains.

external_context.label: Live demo: RL + Control citation graph

title: research-agent
summary: LangGraph research agent on top of research-kb — structured synthesis with citation validation.
description_long: A multi-agent research pipeline built on research-kb: it decomposes a question, retrieves evidence, synthesizes a structured answer, and validates citations against the sources. It's the querying layer of the same enabling system — how I turn the knowledge base into vetted answers.
whats_next: Tightening synthesis quality and wiring outputs back into research-kb for cross-topic reuse.

title: research_toolkit
summary: Claude Code skills for systematic research workflows — literature review, assumption audits, cross-domain concept mapping — built on research-kb + research-agent.
description_long: A collection of Claude Code skills exposing the research-kb and research-agent stack as repeatable workflows: literature review, causal-assumption audits, citation-network analysis, and cross-domain concept discovery. The point is to validate research claims systematically before anything is published.
whats_next: A methodology page on how that validation works — the local research-* dogfooding sandboxes that vet claims before they ship — is coming under this cluster.

title: prompt-injection detection: out-of-distribution (OOD) study
summary: Methodology case study asking whether prompt-injection detectors trained on direct injection still work on attack families they never saw. Written up as a paper, with deployed docs.
description_long: A companion study to the prompt-injection PoC with a different question: do detectors generalize out-of-distribution? Detectors trained on direct-injection attacks are evaluated against unseen attack families (indirect, optimization-based, context-poisoning). The artifact is written up as a research paper — an IMRAD write-up plus a narrative — with CI-checked reproduction and deployed docs. It is a sibling of the existing prompt-injection detector, not a replacement.
whats_next: Findings feed back into the eval-toolkit harness as out-of-distribution robustness test patterns.
